     1|---
     2|title: "Probiotic Target Identification"
     3|params:
     4|  name: "03-probiotic-target-identification"
     5|---
     6|
     7|# Probiotic Target Identification
     8|
     9|## Setup
    10|
    11|```{r setup}
    12|#| label: setup
    13|here::i_am(paste0(params$name, ".qmd"), uuid = "c3d4e5f6-a7b8-9012-cdef-1234567890")
    14|
    15|qproj::proj_create_dir_target(params$name)
    16|path_target <- qproj::path_target(params$name)
    17|path_source <- qproj::path_source(params$name)
    18|
    19|# Read upstream data
    20|path_01 <- qproj::path_source("01-public-data-mining")
    21|path_02 <- qproj::path_source("02-phage-host-prediction")
    22|```
    23|
    24|## Load Packages
    25|
    26|```{r packages}
    27|#| label: packages
    28|library(tidyverse)
    29|library(here)
    30|library(qproj)
    31|library(ggplot2)
    32|```
    33|
    34|## 1. Objective
    35|
    36|Identify which probiotic bacteria benefit most from phage-mediated niche clearing:
    37|
    38|- **Target probiotics**: *Lactobacillus*, *Bifidobacterium*, *Bacillus*, *Enterococcus*
    39|- **Goal**: Understand colonization requirements, receptor usage, metabolic dependencies
    40|- **Outcome**: Rank probiotics by expected synergy with phages
    41|
    42|## 2. Probiotic Colonization Requirements
    43|
    44|### 2.1 Key Factors for Gut Colonization
    45|
    46|Based on NotebookLM literature review:
    47|
    48|| Factor | Description | Relevance to Synergy |
    49||--------|-------------|---------------------|
    50|| Adhesion molecules | Bind to gut epithelial cells | Phage clearing competitors → more adhesion sites |
    51|| Metabolic flexibility | Utilize diverse carbon sources | Survive after pathogen removal |
    52|| Bile tolerance | Survive gut environment | Essential for delivery |
    53|| Competitive exclusion | Outcompete pathogens | Enhanced by phage predation |
    54|| Immune modulation | Regulate host immune response | Complementary to phage action |
    55|
    56|### 2.2 Receptor Landscape Analysis
    57|
    58|Analyze what receptors/pathways probiotics use for colonization.
    59|
    60|```{r receptor-analysis-template}
    61|#| label: receptor-analysis-template
    62|#| eval: false
    63|
    64|# Template: Analyze probiotic genome for adhesion factors
    65|# Key gene families to search:
    66|# - Mub (mucin-binding protein)
    67|# - SpaCBA (sortase-anchored pilus)
    68|# - LBA (lactobacillus adhesin)
    69|# - S-layer proteins
    70|
    71|# Example using eggnog-mapper results (if available)
    72|# probiotic_genes <- read_tsv(file.path(path_02, "processed", "probiotic_annotation.tsv"))
    73|
    74|# Filter for adhesion-related genes
    75|# adhesion_genes <- probiotic_genes %>%
    76|#   filter(grepl("adhesin|mucin|pilus|pilin|S-layer", description, ignore.case = TRUE))
    77|
    78|# knitr::kable(head(adhesion_genes, 10), caption = "Adhesion-Related Genes in Probiotics")
    79|```
    80|
    81|## 3. Niche Competition Modeling
    82|
    83|### 3.1 Ecological Niche Overlap
    84|
    85|Model the competitive relationship between pathogens and probiotics.
    86|
    87|```{r niche-overlap-template}
    88|#| label: niche-overlap-template
    89|#| eval: false
    90|
    91|# Template: Calculate niche overlap index
    92|# Based on:
    93|# - Carbon source utilization profiles
    94|# - Oxygen tolerance
    95|# - pH optima
    96|# - Temperature range
    97|# - Receptor usage
    98|
    99|# Example data structure
   100|niche_data <- tibble(
   101|  species = c("C. perfringens", "L. acidophilus", "S. enterica", "B. subtilis"),
   102|  temp_opt (c(37, 37, 37, 30)),  # Celsius
   103|  pH_opt (c(6.5, 6.0, 7.0, 7.5)),
   104|  oxygen = c("anaerobic", "microaerophilic", "facultative", "aerobic"),
   105|  carbon_sources = c(4, 6, 8, 10)  # Number of carbon sources utilized
   106|)
   107|
   108|# Calculate overlap score (simplified)
   109|# Overlap = 1 / (1 + |temp_diff| + |pH_diff| + |carbon_diff|)
   110|```
   111|
   112|### 3.2 Phage Impact Simulation
   113|
   114|Simulate how phage predation on pathogens opens niche space for probiotics.
   115|
   116|```{r phage-impact-simulation}
   117|#| label: phage-impact-simulation
   118|#| eval: false
   119|
   120|# Template: Simple competition model
   121|# Initial abundances
   122|initial_state <- tibble(
   123|  species = c("Pathogen", "Probiotic", "Commensal1", "Commensal2"),
   124|  abundance = c(100, 10, 50, 40),
   125|  growth_rate = c(1.2, 0.8, 0.9, 0.7),
   126|  susceptibility = c(1.0, 0.0, 0.3, 0.2)  # Phage susceptibility
   127|)
   128|
   129|# Simulation parameters
   130|phage_mortality <- 0.8  # Pathogen mortality due to phage
   131|
   132|# Simulate after phage treatment
   133|post_phage <- initial_state %>%
   134|  mutate(
   135|    new_abundance = ifelse(susceptibility > 0,
   136|      abundance * (1 - susceptibility * phage_mortality),
   137|      abundance * (1 + growth_rate * 0.1))
   138|  )
   139|
   140|# Visualize
   141|# ggplot(post_phage, aes(x = species, y = new_abundance, fill = species)) +
   142|#   geom_bar(stat = "identity") +
   143|#   theme_minimal() +
   144|#   labs(title = "Abundance After Phage Treatment",
   145|#        y = "Relative Abundance", x = "")
   146|```
   147|
   148|## 4. Probiotic Ranking
   149|
   150|### 4.1 Ranking Criteria
   151|
   152|From the Research Master Plan:
   153|
   154|| Criterion | Weight | Description |
   155||-----------|--------|-------------|
   156|| Niche overlap with pathogen | 0.3 | Higher overlap = more benefit from phage |
   157|| Lytic phage availability | 0.25 | Ensure effective phages exist |
   158|| Commercial availability | 0.2 | Ready for application |
   159|| Safety profile | 0.15 | GRAS status, no virulence |
   160|| Mechanism clarity | 0.1 | Understanding of synergy |
   161|
   162|### 4.2 Preliminary Ranking
   163|
   164|*To be populated after analysis*
   165|
   166|| Rank | Probiotic | Target Pathogen | Niche Overlap | Phage Available | Score |
   167||------|------------|-----------------|---------------|------------------|-------|
   168|| 1 | TBD | TBD | - | - | - |
   169|| 2 | TBD | TBD | - | - | - |
   170|| 3 | TBD | TBD | - | - | - |
   171|
   172|## 5. Genomic Analysis
   173|
   174|### 5.1 Probiotic Genome Features
   175|
   176|Analyze probiotic genomes for synergy-relevant features.
   177|
   178|```{r genome-features-template}
   179|#| label: genome-features-template
   180|#| eval: false
   181|
   182|# Template: Analyze probiotic genomes
   183|# Load probiotic genome annotations
   184|# probiotic_gff <- rtracklayer::import.gff3(file.path(path_02, "raw", "probiotic_genomes.gff"))
   185|
   186|# Extract features of interest
   187|# - Bacteriocin genes (competitive advantage)
   188|# - Carbohydrate utilization loci (niche exploitation)
   189|# - Stress response genes (survival)
   190|# - Bile salt hydrolase (gut adaptation)
   191|```
   192|
   193|### 5.2 Comparative Genomics
   194|
   195|Compare probiotic strains for optimal selection.
   196|
   197|```{r comparative-genomics}
   198|#| label: comparative-genomics
   199|#| eval: false
   200|
   201|# Template: Compare multiple strains of same species
   202|# Identify core and accessory genome
   203|# Focus on strains with proven efficacy in animal trials
   204|```
   205|
   206|## 6. NotebookLM Validation
   207|
   208|### 6.1 Query for Probiotic Evidence
   209|
   210|Use NotebookLM to validate probiotic selection.
   211|
   212|```bash
   213|nlm notebook query phage-synergy "Which Lactobacillus strains show best colonization in poultry gut?"
   214|```
   215|
   216|### 6.2 Add Analysis Results
   217|
   218|After generating ranking, add to NotebookLM for literature cross-validation.
   219|
   220|```bash
   221|nlm source add phage-synergy --text "$(cat probiotic_ranking.txt)" --title "Probiotic Ranking pr0117"
   222|```
   223|
   224|## 7. Session Info
   225|
   226|```{r session-info}
   227|#| label: session-info
   228|sessionInfo()
   229|```
   230|
   231|## 8. Next Steps
   232|
   233|1. **Complete genomic analysis** of top probiotic candidates
   234|2. **Finalize ranking** based on multi-criteria assessment
   235|3. **Cross-validate** with NotebookLM literature
   236|4. **Proceed to Module 04** (`04-synergy-scoring.qmd`)
   237|
   238|---
   239|*Generated by qproj workflow for pr0117-PhageProbioticSynergy*
   240|
